era: 1970s. Hard Rock origins. Mushroom Records / Portrait Records. Dreamboat Annie (1975), Little Queen (1977), Magazine (1978), Dog and Butterfly (1978). Crazy on You, Magic Man, Barracuda.
associated_musicians: Led Zeppelin (Robert Plant comparison to Ann Wilson)

theory_nodes.concept: WIDEST S36/SPS E-GAP IN ENGINE. Heart Hard Rock (E=7) + Turkey Vulture (E=2) = E-spread of 5. Record: no prior degree has gap greater than 4.
theory_nodes.description: The maximum S36/SPS energy gap documented at degree 235.

theory_nodes.concept: Ann Wilson at E=7: the Joplin-Plant lineage in hard rock context. Four-octave range deployed at emotional maximum. Crazy on You final chorus as the defining moment.
theory_nodes.description: Ann Wilson's hard rock vocal as primary E=7 document.

theory_nodes.concept: Barracuda as response to exploitation: the Mushroom Records promotional stunt produces one of rock's great protest songs. Authentic anger makes E=7 music.

cross_references.relationship: E=6. Same band, earlier entries. Commercial peak. One energy step separates the two eras.
E=7 continues. FIRST PROGRESSIVE METAL. FIRST BELLEVUE BAND. Bellevue hard rock to Bellevue prog metal.

sps_pair.pairing_note: WIDEST E-GAP IN ENGINE. E=7 + E=2 = spread of 5. Loudest female rock vocal + near-silent bird. FIRST CATHARTIDAE. Both underestimated by their cultural reception; both more powerful than assumed.
